(born October 15, 1979) is a retired American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist"). Uyenoyama formerly competed for the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)"), [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)"), [DEEP](/wiki/Deep_(mixed_martial_arts) "Deep (mixed martial arts)"), and [Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto").

**Darren Uyenoyama** (born October 15, 1979) is a retired American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist"). Uyenoyama formerly competed for the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)"), [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)"), [DEEP](/wiki/Deep_(mixed_martial_arts) "Deep (mixed martial arts)"), and [Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto").

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Uyenoyama was born and raised in San Francisco/South San Francisco area, and is a third-generation Japanese-American. Uyenoyama attended [El Camino High School](/wiki/El_Camino_High_School_(South_San_Francisco) "El Camino High School (South San Francisco)") where he participated in [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling"). He also wrestled at Skyline College and was studying Kinesiology. He began training at [Ralph Gracie](/wiki/Ralph_Gracie "Ralph Gracie")'s [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") academy while attending college. Uyenoyama had a successful career in BJJ, winning tournaments, and eventually transitioned his skills to mixed martial arts.[[2]](#cite_note-mmajunkie-2)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Uyenoyama had his first mixed martial arts fight in 2002 against [Rambaa Somdet](/wiki/Rambaa_Somdet "Rambaa Somdet"), and although he did win his debut, he took a five-year hiatus before returning with another win.

### DREAM

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: DREAM")]

Uyenoyama made his [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)") debut against [Hideo Tokoro](/wiki/Hideo_Tokoro "Hideo Tokoro") and lost the fight via unanimous decision.

### Strikeforce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Strikeforce")]

Uyenoyama made his [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") debut, defeating Andrew Valladerez via a [rear-naked choke](/wiki/Rear-naked_choke "Rear-naked choke") submission in 59 seconds at [Strikeforce: Young Guns](/wiki/Strikeforce:_Young_Guns "Strikeforce: Young Guns").

Uyenoyama moved his Strikeforce record to 2-0 with another first-round submission win with a [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke") over Anthony Figueroa.

Uyenoyama's third and final Strikeforce appearance took place at [Strikeforce: Destruction](/wiki/Strikeforce:_Destruction "Strikeforce: Destruction"). Uyenoyama won via unanimous decision.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Uyenoyama signed with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") in 2011.

Uyenoyama made his bantamweight debut against Japanese superstar [Norifumi Yamamoto](/wiki/Norifumi_Yamamoto "Norifumi Yamamoto") at [UFC on Fox: Velasquez vs. Dos Santos](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Velasquez_vs._Dos_Santos "UFC on Fox: Velasquez vs. Dos Santos").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Uyenoyama defeated heavy favorite Yamamoto via unanimous decision.

Uyenoyama was expected to make his Flyweight debut at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3") on May 5, 2012, against [John Dodson](/wiki/John_Dodson_(fighter) "John Dodson (fighter)"). However, Uyenoyama was forced out of the bout and replaced by promotional newcomer [Tim Elliott](/wiki/Tim_Elliott "Tim Elliott").[[2]](#cite_note-mmajunkie-2)

Uyenoyama was expected to face [Louis Gaudinot](/wiki/Louis_Gaudinot "Louis Gaudinot") on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[4]](#cite_note-4) However, Gaudinot was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by promotional newcomer [Phil Harris](/wiki/Phil_Harris_(fighter) "Phil Harris (fighter)").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Uyenoyama submitted Harris in the second round with a rear-naked choke.

Uyenoyama faced [Joseph Benavidez](/wiki/Joseph_Benavidez "Joseph Benavidez") on April 20, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 7](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_7 "UFC on Fox 7").[[6]](#cite_note-6) Uyenoyama lost the fight via TKO in the second round.

Uyenoyama was expected to face [John Moraga](/wiki/John_Moraga "John Moraga") on December 14, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_9 "UFC on Fox 9").[[7]](#cite_note-7) However, Moraga pulled out of the bout with an undisclosed injury and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Alptekin Özkiliç](/wiki/Alptekin_%C3%96zkili%C3%A7 "Alptekin Özkiliç").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Uyenoyama lost the fight via split decision, and was subsequently released from the promotion shortly after.

### Post-UFC career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darren_Uyenoyama&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Post-UFC career")]

Following his release from the UFC, Uyenoyama signed with Pacific Xtreme Combat in September 2014. He faced Shane Alvarez at PXC 45 on October 24, 2014, and won the fight via ground-and-pound TKO in the first round.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

On March 13, 2015, he faced Kentaro Watanabe at PXC 47, winning the fight via split decision.

Uyenoyama challenged Riley Dutro for the vacant PXC [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") title on November 18, 2016, at PXC 55, losing the fight by TKO (punches) in the first round.
